global.name: Anay Gawate
global.headline: CSE Student | Building apps that should exist | Flutter · Embedded · Self-taught since 14

homePage.tagline: I build fast, beautiful web apps. I am dedicated to crafting innovative solutions and continuously learning in the evolving tech landscape. Here’s my work!
homePage.linkedinTagline: CSE Student | Building apps that should exist | Flutter · Embedded · Self-taught since 14
homePage.bio: I am currently pursuing a B.Tech in Computer Science. I thrive on building real stuff that works, from full-stack web apps to AI Interfaces. Whether it's solving technical puzzles, designing for humans, or digging through data, I'm all in. I’m here to contribute to teams that build great products.
aboutExtended: I've been building things on the internet since I was 14 — starting with Android apps in Java/Kotlin, crashing RC planes, and figuring out how computers actually work.

Currently a CS student, but most of what I know came from just doing things. I built a full cross-platform nutrition tracker in Flutter because the good ones cost money and I didn't think they should.
It runs on Android, iOS and web, uses Firebase for auth and database, and has a crowdsourced food database I've been building myself.
I work across the stack — Flutter, C/C++, Python, Java, web frontend, embedded systems, Linux, local LLMs.
I've deployed on Vercel, Netlify and Cloudflare, run models locally with Ollama and LM Studio, and I know my way around hardware as well as software.
Open to technical internships where I can actually build something.

experience.title: Android Developer
experience.description: Independently developed and published Android applications from age 15. First app reached 10K+ organic downloads on the Google Play Store. Continued shipping across Android, iOS, and Web, including a Flutter-based nutrition and health tracker backed by Firebase.

education.degree: Bachelor of Technology - BTech, Computer Science
education.description: Currently pursuing undergraduate studies in Computer Science while building cross-platform apps and systems projects.

projectsPage.title: My Projects
projectsPage.subtitle: A collection of my work, from full-stack web applications and robust desktop tools to interactive front-end experiments. Each project reflects my commitment to clean code, thoughtful design, and practical problem-solving.

allProjects.title: PureFuel
allProjects.description: A cross-platform nutrition and health tracker for Android, iOS, and Web built as a free, ad-free alternative to paid apps. Includes Firebase auth/database and a crowdsourced food database.

allProjects.title: Journease - Travel Planner
allProjects.description: A full-featured app for all your travel needs, with a focus on a clean UI and efficient backend processing.

allProjects.title: School Management System (In Development)
allProjects.description: Building a full-featured cross-platform school management application in Flutter for a private school, with broad operational and data management features.

skillsPage.title: My Toolkit
skillsPage.subtitle: I believe in using the right tool for the job. Here's a look at the languages, frameworks, and technologies I work with to build efficient, scalable, and beautiful applications.

volunteering.organization: School of Computing, MIT ADT University
volunteering.description: Primary point of contact between 70+ students and faculty for 2 semesters in FY.
